description: Refutation fixture: spec cites a fabricated standard designation ('NFPA 1300') that does not exist; verifier correctly DISPUTED it, and the model's proposed replacement is itself uncertain, so a human must resolve.

finding.issue: Specification cites 'NFPA 1300 Standard for Data Center Suppression' as the governing standard for the clean-agent system; no such NFPA standard exists.

paragraph_map_slice.text: A. Provide clean-agent suppression per NFPA 1300.

captured_verifier_response.explanation: There is no NFPA 1300. The applicable clean-agent standard is NFPA 2001 (Clean Agent Fire Extinguishing Systems); however, depending on the room use the author may have intended NFPA 75/76 or NFPA 855, so the single replacement cannot be asserted with confidence. The citation is fabricated and must be corrected by a human.

ground_truth.notes: NFPA 1300 is a fabricated designation. Refutation is where verification earns its cost; the model's single replacement is uncertain, so DISPUTED (not CORRECTED) is right.
